title: RPYR-0004 — `mmap.find`/`rfind` slice-panic when `start > end`: `get_find_range` clamps each bound but not their order (`mmap.rs:795`/`812`)
guarded_twin: the fix belongs in get_find_range itself — a single `end = end.max(start)` (or clamp start = start.min(end)); the read/read_byte/readline accessors in the same file DO clamp their bounds
notes: One root cause, two Python-facing methods. get_find_range (mmap.rs:774) computes start and end each via saturated_at(size), which independently clamps each bound to [0, size] but never enforces start <= end. When start > end, &slice[start..end] panics. Both find (795) and rfind (812) call get_find_range. Reproduced on the binary; find also triggers via the pos-default path: m.seek(9); m.find(b'x', None, 2).
